topic: Theme 3: Immigration and the German Multicultural Society
subject: german

items.why: Spelled identically to English 'bald' but means SOON. 'Bis bald!' = 'See you soon!'. The English 'bald' translates as 'kahl' or 'glatzköpfig'. Pure faux ami.

items.correct: A soft palatal fricative (ich-laut), made at the front of the mouth.
items.why: Students substitute English 'ch'. German 'ch' has two sounds: ICH-laut (soft, front of mouth, after i/e/ä/ö/ü) and ACH-laut (hard, back of throat, after a/o/u). Neither matches English 'church'.

items.q: What is the imperative 'du' form of 'machen' (to do)?
items.why: Students think you keep the present tense form. The du-imperative typically drops the -en or -st: 'Mach das!' (do it!), 'Komm!' (come!). The pronoun du is also normally omitted.

items.q: Which case follows 'wegen' in formal German?
items.why: Correct answer: genitive. “nominative” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's Merkel. “Kohl” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In which year was 'Wir schaffen das' spoken?
items.why: The real figure is 2015. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
